data.question: What is our company policy on remote work?
data.retrieved_context: Section 4.2 (Remote Work): Employees are eligible for remote work after 90 days of employment. Remote-eligible roles must work in-office at least 2 days per week unless granted a fully-remote exception by their manager.
data.answer: Our remote work policy says you become eligible after 90 days of employment. Remote-eligible roles need to be in the office at least 2 days a week, unless your manager grants a fully-remote exception.

data.question: How many vacation days do new employees get?
data.retrieved_context: Section 6.1 (PTO): All full-time employees accrue paid time off at 1.25 days per month, totaling 15 days per year. PTO begins accruing on the first day of employment.
data.answer: New employees get 20 vacation days per year, accrued over time.

data.question: Can I expense my home internet?
data.retrieved_context: Section 8.4 (Expense Reimbursement): The following are reimbursable for fully-remote employees: a one-time home office stipend up to $500, monthly internet reimbursement up to $50, and ergonomic equipment as approved by the manager. Hybrid employees are not eligible for monthly internet reimbursement.
data.answer: If you're a fully-remote employee, yes - up to $50 per month. Hybrid employees aren't eligible for that one. There's also a one-time $500 home office stipend if you're fully remote.

data.question: What's the parental leave policy?
data.retrieved_context: Section 6.3 (Parental Leave): Birthing parents receive 16 weeks of paid leave. Non-birthing parents receive 8 weeks of paid leave. Both must be taken within 12 months of the birth or adoption.
data.answer: Birthing parents get 16 weeks of paid leave. Non-birthing parents get 8 weeks. You can take it any time within the first 12 months after birth or adoption.

data.question: Are my medical records confidential?
data.answer: Yes, all medical records are kept strictly confidential under HIPAA and our internal data handling policy.
